area: Unit Content - Person of Jesus Christ
descripcion: I can comprehend and respond to Christ's first and second advent (projects, outreach, research etc.).
I can identify and examine Scripture as the foundational source of revealing of God, taking into account its origin and purpose.
I can explore and evaluate Seventh-day Adventist biblical fundamental beliefs.
I can apply Seventh-day Adventist beliefs in discipleship of Christ through relationships, lifestyle, and service.
I can explore and examine the life of Ellen G. White as a prophet of God and utilize her writings.

I can develop a wide range of techniques for studying the Bible as its own expositor and incorporate its relevance from past to present through studying Ellen White's writings and other contemporary and historical documents.
I can acquire and integrate knowledge to research and analyze biblical stories and/or spiritual concepts, in some depth.
I can develop creative and higher order thinking skills (research, analysis, synthesis, evaluation, etc.) based on a biblical worldview.

I can use verbal and nonverbal responses to convey a clear, engaging message across a range of communication types about biblical spirituality to meet the needs of specific contexts, purposes, and audiences.
I can develop the skills to give reasons to support one's thinking, applying prior knowledge to new contexts, in order to clarify one's own personal, biblical position.
I can analyze and apply the relevance of biblical and historical themes and apply this to one's life.
I can identify and explore ways in which one's relationship with God and His Word is life changing and is expressed through knowledge, attitudes and actions.

I can learn relationship-building skills by applying Biblical, Christ-like principles of genuine care for others, handling challenges in human relationships, communicating effectively, accepting responsibility, participating in community, and serving others.
I can identify that God's relationship with humankind crosses cultures and borders and embraces all humanity.
I can research the nature of God as seen in Old Testament times and how He related to His people.
I can define and outline a covenant relationship, specifically between God and His people.
I can analyze the role and messages of some of the Old Testament prophets, specifically Hosea, in light of the people's obedience, destruction, and deliverance.

I can investigate key messages from the story of Hosea such as the role of knowledge in relationship, obedience, idolatry, sin, grace, healthy relationships, and responding to the generous heart of God.
I can identify characteristics of David that made him a man after God's own heart.
I can apply these characteristics to how one can live after God's heart today.
I can explore key events surrounding the life of David, such as his anointing, his battle with Goliath, fleeing from Saul, Saul's death, David's kingship, and his adultery with Bathsheba.

I can investigate key messages from the life of David, such as what it means to be called by God; humility and pride; temptation and sin; and responding to God's love, grace, and desire for a relationship with us; and apply these themes to one's life.

descripcion: I can personally apply the messages from the Sermon on the Mount in relevant and meaningful ways.

descripcion: I can investigate what Jesus meant when He taught about the kingdom of God.
